description: Static golden example for the cross-binding conservative-regridding geometry tolerance contract (CONFORMANCE_SPEC.md §5.8, RFC semiring-faq-unified-ir §8.1 + Appendix B.5). This is the tolerance-mode analogue of tests/conformance/determinism/: the ONE byte-identical anchor is the integer bin-Skolem candidate overlap-pair index set (the broad-phase spatial join, §5.8.5, governed by the §5.5 determinism contract); the physical invariants (global conservation + partition-of-unity, §5.8.3) are the PRIMARY gate; per-pair overlap areas / weights are compared with a combined relative + absolute tolerance and a sliver floor (§5.8.2). Each producing binding (Julia / Python; Rust folds in once its S2 FFI lands) ships a thin adapter (scripts/run-geometry-conformance.py + $EARTHSCI_GEOMETRY_ADAPTER_<BINDING>) and the runner asserts: (1) the candidate set is byte-identical to this golden after base normalization — for the canonical input AND the adversarial permuted-input-order variant, which MUST collapse to the identical set; (2) partition-of-unity holds to a tight epsilon and, where the target tiles the source domain, conservation holds within tolerance; (3) per-pair areas agree with the golden (and, when ≥2 bindings run together, with the reference binding) within atol + rtol·A_ref, sub-atol slivers treated as zero. The embedded reference implementation (pure-Python bin-Skolem broad phase + planar Sutherland–Hodgman clip + shoelace area) is the --self-test green check; spherical areas are S2/GeometryOps-derived and gated cross-binding rather than against a hand golden.

base_pin._doc: candidate_overlap_pairs returns (src_idx, tgt_idx) positional indices into the input arrays. Julia is 1-based, Python is 0-based. Conformance asserts on the CANONICAL 0-based numbering; each adapter declares its base and the harness normalizes via (reported - base) before serializing/comparing. Rust (when it lands) is 0-based.

tolerances._doc: The spec pins only atol ≈ 1e-15·R² (the sliver floor); rtol is empirically calibrated and the conservation tolerance is application-set, so these are documented defaults the runner uses unless a fixture overrides area_rtol. atol per fixture = area_atol_factor · characteristic_length². partition-of-unity is exact by construction (tight epsilon).

fixtures.doc: Conservative coarsening: four unit source cells tiling [0,2]² remap onto one [0,2]² target cell. Every source is fully contained, so A_i0 = 1, A_j = 4, W_i0 = 0.25, partition-of-unity is exact, and conservation is exact (the target tiles the source domain). The candidate set is all four (i,0).

Adversarial tangent slivers inside a perfect tiling. Source {S0=[0,1]², S1=[1,2]×[0,1]} and target {T0=[0,1]², T1=[1,2]×[0,1]} share the column-1 spatial bins, so the candidate set is all four (i,j) — including the edge-adjacent pairs (0,1) and (1,0) whose true overlap is a zero-area line. Those slivers MUST floor to zero (§5.8.2); the surviving set is the diagonal. partition-of-unity and conservation are both exact. The reversed_order variant permutes both grids and MUST collapse to the identical candidate set after the index remap (§5.8.5).

A single source cell [0,1]² and a single target cell [0.5,1.5]² overlapping in the [0.5,1]² quarter (area 0.25). Exercises a genuine fractional area through the tolerance check; partition-of-unity is still exact (W_00 = 1). Conservation is NOT asserted — the target sticks out of the source domain, so mass is legitimately not conserved (§5.8.3).

A source cell [0,1]² and a far-away target cell [5,6]² occupy disjoint spatial bins, so the broad phase produces NO candidates at all — the empty index set. Validates that the bin-Skolem join correctly excludes non-overlapping cells before any clipping (§5.8.5: candidate set is a superset of the surviving set, but disjoint cells are not even candidates).

Pole-touching spherical cells: two lon-sectors {[0,90], [90,180]} × lat [80,90] whose top edge collapses to the north pole (great-circle edges, the spherical-clip-not-planar-clip regime), remapped onto the union sector. The candidate set is manifold-independent (integer lon/lat binning) and reference-reproducible; the overlap areas are spherical excess, so they are gated CROSS-BINDING (Julia/GeometryOps vs Python/S2) within the §5.8.2 tolerance rather than against a hand golden. partition-of-unity is exact by construction regardless of the edge model (§5.8.3). The permuted variant MUST collapse to the identical candidate set.

negative_controls.doc: A candidate set delivered in producer/hash order rather than sorted total order MUST still canonicalize to the golden (the harness re-sorts after base normalization — order-independence is the contract). Validated in --self-test by feeding a rotated-but-complete set and asserting it passes, while an INCOMPLETE set is rejected.

A candidate set missing a true candidate pair MUST be rejected — the broad phase is complete (a superset of the surviving set), so a dropped candidate is a conformance failure. Validated in --self-test.

A float component in a spatial-bin key is forbidden (§5.8.5) — keys are integers minted by floor(). A producer that leaks a raw coordinate into a key MUST raise rather than bucket on a platform-dependent float repr. Validated in --self-test.
An overlap area outside atol + rtol·A_ref MUST be flagged. Validated in --self-test by perturbing a golden area by 1.0 and asserting rejection — a harness that cannot detect a violation is worthless.
A partition-of-unity residual above the tight epsilon MUST be flagged — Σ_i W_ij = 1 is exact by construction, so any real deviation is a producer bug. Validated in --self-test.
